drmd:description.dcc:content: Boric acid in water. ERM-AE120 is composed of an aqueous boric acid solution and is filled in PFA-bottles of approximately 20 mL, sealed in a plastic bag. It is designed for calibration and validation of all procedures (e.g. TIMS, ICPMS) being used for the determination of δ¹¹B-values.

1) δ¹¹B is a measure for the isotope variation. It is expressed as the shift of the isotopic composition relative to an internationally accepted standard given in per mill. It is calculated according to the following equation, with NIST SRM 951 (isotope reference material for boron) being used as reference: δ¹¹B = ((Rsample/Rreference)-1). This certified reference material (CRM) is traceable to the international d-scale for boron with the origin being represented by NIST SRM 951. 2) Expanded uncertainty U with a coverage factor of k = 2, as defined in the Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement (GUM), including the repeatability of the measurement and of the determination of correction factors for systematic errors as well as contributions from certified values.

1) The isotopic composition is being determined by TIMS and is given as indicative value. The boron mass fraction was determined by isotope dilution mass spectrometry and gravimetry and is considered as indicative value only. It is traceable to the International System of units (SI) in the shortest possible way. Every measurement and correction is being calibrated using SI traceable calibrators. 2) Expanded uncertainty U with a coverage factor of k = 2, as defined in the Guide to the Expression of Uncertainty in Measurement (GUM) and includes the repeatability of the measurement and of the determination of correction factors for systematic errors as well as contributions from certified values. The uncertainty value is traceable to the SI.

dcc:name.dcc:content: Molar mass of Boron in solution M(B)
Mass fraction of boron in solution, w(B)

drmd:description.dcc:content: The certified values with their combined standard uncertainties (k = 1) are given in the following table:

drmd:intendedUse.dcc:content: ERM-AE120 is composed of an aqueous boric acid solution and is filled in PFA-bottles of approximately 20 mL, sealed in a plastic bag. It is designed for calibration and validation of all procedures (e.g. TIMS, ICPMS) being used for the determination of δ¹¹B-values.

drmd:storageInformation.dcc:content: This CRM should be stored under cool (5 ± 3) °C and dark conditions to reduce evaporation effects. However, BAM cannot be held responsible for changes that happen during storage of the material at the customer's premises, especially of opened samples.

drmd:instructionsForHandlingAndUse.dcc:content: The solution should be withdrawn by pouring in another bottle or container and never by pipettes and the like. Any contamination will result in a bias of the δ¹¹B value.

drmd:metrologicalTraceability.dcc:content: This certified reference material (CRM) is traceable to the international d-scale for boron with the origin being represented by NIST SRM 951. The isotopic composition is being determined by TIMS and is given as indicative value. The boron mass fraction was determined by isotope dilution mass spectrometry and gravimetry and is considered as indicative value only. It is traceable to the International System of units (SI) in the shortest possible way. Every measurement and correction is being calibrated using SI traceable calibrators. The uncertainty value is traceable to the SI.

drmd:referenceToCertificationReport.dcc:content: A detailed technical report describing the production, characterisation as well as the analytical procedures applied and the treatment of the analytical data used to certify ERM®-AE120 is available on request or can be downloaded from BAM website (www.bam.de).

drmd:healthAndSafetyInformation.dcc:content: The usual laboratory safety precautions apply.

drmd:legalNotice.dcc:content: Neither BAM, its contractors nor any person acting on their behalf: (a) make any warranty or representation, express or implied, that the use of any information, material, apparatus, method or process disclosed in this document does not infringe any privately owned intellectual property rights; or (b) assume any liability with respect to, or for damages resulting from, the use of any information, material, apparatus, method or process disclosed in this document save for loss or damage arising solely and directly from the negligence of BAM.

drmd:digitalReferenceMaterialDocument.drmd:comment: For additional information please refer to the pdf certificate
